title: The Spirit of Counsel
reading:
There is a kind of knowledge that stays theoretical — principles you understand but haven't yet received as personal direction. The Spirit of Counsel moves knowledge from principle to instruction. It is the voice that says: This is the way. Walk in it.

Moses was gifted, called, and fully committed to God's purpose. But in Exodus 18, his father-in-law Jethro watched him exhaust himself by handling every decision alone. The counsel Moses received that day — delegate, appoint leaders, share the load — was not supernatural in the conventional sense. It was practical wisdom given through a human voice, but it was the Spirit of Counsel at work. Not every instance of divine direction feels miraculous. Some of it comes through someone wise enough to see what you cannot.

David's pattern throughout his life was to inquire of the Lord before battle. In 1 Samuel 23:2, facing the Philistines at Keilah, David asks: "Shall I go and attack?" God answers specifically. David's strength was not just his courage — it was his consistent posture of seeking direction before acting.

Jehoshaphat's story in 2 Chronicles 20 is one of the most striking examples of the Spirit of Counsel in Scripture. Surrounded by three armies with no military strategy available, he calls the nation to fast and seek God. The word comes: "You will not need to fight in this battle. Position yourselves, stand still and see the salvation of the LORD." The counsel was counterintuitive. But it was specific, and it was enough.

The Spirit of Counsel does not always explain the full plan. It gives the next step. And when you take it, the next step after that becomes visible.
reflection:
Is there a decision in front of you where you have been relying on logic alone rather than seeking God's specific direction?

Think of a time God gave you clear direction through an unexpected source — another person, a verse, a circumstance. How did you recognize it?

What practice could you build into your decision-making that creates space for the Spirit of Counsel to speak?
abide: Lord, I don't want to move without You. Before I act, before I plan, before I decide — speak to me. Give me ears to hear Your counsel and the courage to follow it even when it doesn't make sense from the outside. I want to be someone who consistently asks before acting, knowing that You always answer those who seek You.
